evals.prompt: I've got the Meta-MCP server set up in Claude Desktop. I want to build an MCP server that wraps our internal REST API (base URL: https://internal.mycompany.com/api, needs an Authorization header with a Bearer token). Walk me through using the wizard tools.
evals.expected_output: Guidance on using wizard_chat to describe the API, iterating until is_ready, then going through wizard_start, wizard_list_tools, wizard_submit_tools, wizard_list_env_vars, wizard_submit_env_vars, wizard_set_auth, wizard_generate_code, wizard_deploy. Should mention the polling pattern for async tool calls.

assertions.text: Explains iterating wizard_chat until is_ready: true
Correctly identifies async tools (wizard_start, wizard_generate_code) requiring polling
Explains calling wizard_state and checking processing_status
Mentions env vars will be suggested for the API base URL and bearer token credential
Shows {uuid: value} dict format for wizard_submit_env_vars

evals.prompt: How do I connect the MCPHero Meta-MCP server to Claude Desktop?
evals.expected_output: Instructions to add the Meta-MCP server URL to claude_desktop_config.json with the correct mcpServers JSON structure.

assertions.text: Shows mcpServers JSON block with the meta-mcp URL https://api.mcphero.app/mcp/meta/mcp
Mentions OAuth authentication is handled automatically

evals.prompt: I just deployed an MCP server with MCPHero. The deploy returned server_url "/mcp/abc-123/mcp" and bearer_token "sk-xyz". How do I connect it to Claude Desktop?
evals.expected_output: Instructions to add the deployed server to claude_desktop_config.json with the full URL (https://api.mcphero.app/mcp/abc-123/mcp) and the bearer token as an Authorization header.

assertions.text: Constructs the full URL by prepending https://api.mcphero.app to the relative server_url
Shows mcpServers config with Authorization header using the bearer token
Uses the exact server path /mcp/abc-123/mcp in the config
